rationale: The citing paper discusses Park, Jun, and MacInnis (2000) in the core theory section as prior evidence of loss-versus-gain framing within bundled option decisions. It then explicitly positions its own contribution as separating the effect of bundling itself from that prior loss/gain framing contrast, and uses this distinction to motivate hypotheses and study design. This is a meaningful conceptual use, not a passing background citation.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Park et al. (2000) as a key antecedent on option-framing and loss aversion in bundle contexts, then narrows the question by isolating bundling-versus-separate presentation effects. In doing so, it refines the prior framing by distinguishing incremental bundling effects from the loss-vs-gain effect documented in the cited study.
